question_text: Which statement correctly describes diffraction of waves?

options.label: A
options.text: Diffraction occurs when waves bounce off a barrier and change direction

options.label: B
options.text: Diffraction occurs when waves bend around obstacles or through narrow gaps

options.label: C
options.text: Diffraction is when waves change speed as they travel through different materials

options.label: D
options.text: Diffraction only happens with sound waves, not with light or water waves

explanation: Diffraction is the bending of waves around obstacles or through narrow openings, and it occurs with all types of waves. Option B correctly defines this phenomenon; option A describes reflection, and option C describes refraction.
